children.heading: Authorization to conduct in subsidiaries certain activities that are financial in nature
children.label: a Authorization to conduct in subsidiaries certain activities that are financial in nature

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), a national bank may control a financial subsidiary, or hold an interest in a financial subsidiary.

children.label: i
children.text: activities that are financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity pursuant to subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: activities that are permitted for national banks to engage in directly (subject to the same terms and conditions that govern the conduct of the activities by a national bank);

children.label: i
children.text: insuring, guaranteeing, or indemnifying against loss, harm, damage, illness, disability, or death (except to the extent permitted under section 302 or 303(c) of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act [ or 6713(c)]) or providing or issuing annuities the income of which is subject to tax treatment under ;15 U.S.C. 6712section 72 of title 26

children.label: ii
children.text: real estate development or real estate investment activities, unless otherwise expressly authorized by law; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any activity permitted in subparagraph (H) or (I) of , except activities described in that may be permitted in accordance with section 122 of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act;section 1843(k)(4) of this titlesection 1843(k)(4)(H) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the national bank and each depository institution affiliate of the national bank are well capitalized and well managed;

children.label: i
children.text: 45 percent of the consolidated total assets of the parent bank; or

children.label: E
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (4), the national bank meets standards of credit-worthiness established by the Comptroller of the Currency or other requirement set forth in paragraph (3); and

children.label: F
children.text: the national bank has received the approval of the Comptroller of the Currency for the financial subsidiary to engage in such activities, which approval shall be based solely upon the factors set forth in this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A national bank meets the requirements of this paragraph if the bank is one of the 100 largest insured banks and has not fewer than 1 issue of outstanding debt that meets standards of credit-worthiness or other criteria as the Secretary of the Treasury and the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System may jointly establish.

children.heading: Consolidated total assets
children.label: B Consolidated total assets
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the size of an insured bank shall be determined on the basis of the consolidated total assets of the bank as of the end of each calendar year.

children.heading: Financial agency subsidiary
children.label: 4 Financial agency subsidiary
children.text: The requirement in paragraph (2)(E) shall not apply with respect to the ownership or control of a financial subsidiary that engages in activities described in subsection (b)(1) solely as agent and not directly or indirectly as principal.

children.heading: Regulations required
children.label: 5 Regulations required
children.text: Before the end of the 270-day period beginning on , the Comptroller of the Currency shall, by regulation, prescribe procedures to implement this section.November 12, 1999

children.heading: Indexed asset limit
children.label: 6 Indexed asset limit
children.text: The dollar amount contained in paragraph (2)(D) shall be adjusted according to an indexing mechanism jointly established by regulation by the Secretary of the Treasury and the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.

children.heading: Coordination withsection 1843(l)(2) of this title
children.label: 7 Coordination withsection 1843(l)(2) of this title
children.text: Section 1843()(2) of this title applies to a national bank that controls a financial subsidiary in the manner provided in that section.l

children.heading: Activities that are financial in nature
children.label: b Activities that are financial in nature
i
children.text: such activity has been defined to be financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity for bank holding companies pursuant to ; orsection 1843(k)(4) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary of the Treasury determines the activity is financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Coordination between the Board and the Secretary of the Treasury
children.label: B Coordination between the Board and the Secretary of the Treasury

children.heading: Proposals raised before the Secretary of the Treasury
children.label: i Proposals raised before the Secretary of the Treasury

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: I Consultation
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall notify the Board of, and consult with the Board concerning, any request, proposal, or application under this section for a determination of whether an activity is financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity.

children.heading: Board view
children.label: II Board view
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall not determine that any activity is financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity under this section if the Board notifies the Secretary in writing, not later than 30 days after the date of receipt of the notice described in subclause (I) (or such longer period as the Secretary determines to be appropriate under the circumstances) that the Board believes that the activity is not financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity or is not otherwise permissible under this section.

children.heading: Proposals raised by the Board
children.label: ii Proposals raised by the Board

children.heading: Board recommendation
children.label: I Board recommendation
children.text: The Board may, at any time, recommend in writing that the Secretary of the Treasury find an activity to be financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Time period for secretarial action
children.label: II Time period for secretarial action
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of receipt of a written recommendation from the Board under subclause (I) (or such longer period as the Secretary of the Treasury and the Board determine to be appropriate under the circumstances), the Secretary shall determine whether to initiate a public rulemaking proposing that the subject recommended activity be found to be financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity under this section, and shall notify the Board in writing of the determination of the Secretary and, in the event that the Secretary determines not to seek public comment on the proposal, the reasons for that determination.

children.label: A
children.text: the purposes of this Act and the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act;1So in original.1

children.label: B
children.text: changes or reasonably expected changes in the marketplace in which banks compete;

children.label: C
children.text: changes or reasonably expected changes in the technology for delivering financial services; and

children.label: i
children.text: compete effectively with any company seeking to provide financial services in the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: efficiently deliver information and services that are financial in nature through the use of technological means, including any application necessary to protect the security or efficacy of systems for the transmission of data or financial transactions; and

children.label: iii
children.text: offer customers any available or emerging technological means for using financial services or for the document imaging of data.

children.label: A
children.text: Lending, exchanging, transferring, investing for others, or safeguarding financial assets other than money or securities.

children.label: B
children.text: Providing any device or other instrumentality for transferring money or other financial assets.

children.label: C
children.text: Arranging, effecting, or facilitating financial transactions for the account of third parties.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate amount of the outstanding equity investment, including retained earnings, of a national bank in all financial subsidiaries shall be deducted from the assets and tangible equity of the national bank; and

children.label: B
children.text: the assets and liabilities of the financial subsidiaries shall not be consolidated with those of the national bank.

children.heading: Financial statement disclosure of capital deduction
children.label: 2 Financial statement disclosure of capital deduction
children.text: Any published financial statement of a national bank that controls a financial subsidiary shall, in addition to providing information prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, separately present financial information for the bank in the manner provided in paragraph (1).

the procedures of the national bank for identifying and managing financial and operational risks within the national bank and the financial subsidiary adequately protect the national bank from such risks;

the national bank has, for the protection of the bank, reasonable policies and procedures to preserve the separate corporate identity and limited liability of the national bank and the financial subsidiaries of the national bank; and
the national bank is in compliance with this section.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to national banks that fail to continue to meet certain requirements
children.label: e Provisions applicable to national banks that fail to continue to meet certain requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If a national bank or insured depository institution affiliate does not continue to meet the requirements of subsection (a)(2)(C) or subsection (d), the Comptroller of the Currency shall promptly give notice to the national bank to that effect describing the conditions giving rise to the notice.

children.heading: Agreement to correct conditions
children.label: 2 Agreement to correct conditions
children.text: Not later than 45 days after the date of receipt by a national bank of a notice given under paragraph (1) (or such additional period as the Comptroller of the Currency may permit), the national bank shall execute an agreement with the Comptroller of the Currency and any relevant insured depository institution affiliate shall execute an agreement with its appropriate Federal banking agency to comply with the requirements of subsection (a)(2)(C) and subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: the Comptroller of the Currency may impose such limitations on the conduct or activities of the national bank or any subsidiary of the national bank as the Comptroller of the Currency determines to be appropriate under the circumstances and consistent with the purposes of this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the appropriate Federal banking agency may impose such limitations on the conduct or activities of any relevant insured depository institution affiliate or any subsidiary of the institution as such agency determines to be appropriate under the circumstances and consistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Failure to correct
children.label: 4 Failure to correct
children.text: If the conditions described in a notice to a national bank under paragraph (1) are not corrected within 180 days after the date of receipt by the national bank of the notice, the Comptroller of the Currency may require the national bank, under such terms and conditions as may be imposed by the Comptroller and subject to such extension of time as may be granted in the discretion of the Comptroller, to divest control of any financial subsidiary.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 5 Consultation
children.text: In taking any action under this subsection, the Comptroller shall consult with all relevant Federal and State regulatory agencies and authorities.

children.heading: Failure to meet standards of credit-worthiness meet applicable criteria2So in original. Probably should be “or meet”.2
children.label: f Failure to meet standards of credit-worthiness meet applicable criteria2So in original. Probably should be “or meet”.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A national bank that does not continue to meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Comptroller of the Currency or other requirement of subsection (a)(2)(E) after acquiring or establishing a financial subsidiary shall not, directly or through a subsidiary, purchase or acquire any additional equity capital of any financial subsidiary until the bank meets such requirements.

children.heading: Equity capital
children.label: 2 Equity capital
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “equity capital” includes, in addition to any equity instrument, any debt instrument issued by a financial subsidiary, if the instrument qualifies as capital of the subsidiary under any Federal or State law, regulation, or interpretation applicable to the subsidiary.

children.heading: Affiliate, company, control, and subsidiary
children.label: 1 Affiliate, company, control, and subsidiary
children.text: The terms “affiliate”, “company”, “control”, and “subsidiary” have the meanings given those terms in .section 1841 of this title

children.heading: Appropriate Federal banking agency, depository institution, insured bank, and insured depository institution
children.label: 2 Appropriate Federal banking agency, depository institution, insured bank, and insured depository institution
children.text: The terms “appropriate Federal banking agency”, “depository institution”, “insured bank”, and “insured depository institution” have the meanings given those terms in .section 1813 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: engages solely in activities that national banks are permitted to engage in directly and are conducted subject to the same terms and conditions that govern the conduct of such activities by national banks; or

children.label: B
children.text: a national bank is specifically authorized by the express terms of a Federal statute (other than this section), and not by implication or interpretation, to control, such as by section 25 or 25A of the Federal Reserve Act [ et seq., 611 et seq.] or the Bank Service Company Act [ et seq.].12 U.S.C. 60112 U.S.C. 1861

children.label: A
children.text: is not supported by any form of credit enhancement, including a guarantee or standby letter of credit; and

children.label: B
children.text: is not held in whole or in any significant part by any affiliate, officer, director, principal shareholder, or employee of the bank or any other person acting on behalf of or with funds from the bank or an affiliate of the bank.

children.heading: Well capitalized
children.label: 5 Well capitalized
children.text: The term “well capitalized” has the meaning given the term in section 1831 of this title.o

children.label: i
children.text: the achievement of a composite rating of 1 or 2 under the Uniform Financial Institutions Rating System (or an equivalent rating under an equivalent rating system) in connection with the most recent examination or subsequent review of the depository institution; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at least a rating of 2 for management, if such rating is given; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any depository institution that has not been examined, the existence and use of managerial resources that the appropriate Federal banking agency determines are satisfactory.
